title: Hello, World!
description: Write and run your first Python program.
recap: You wrote your first Python program using print(). Strings go inside quotes, and print() displays them on screen.
practicePrompts: Can you print your name on one line and your favorite color on the next?
Try printing a sentence that includes both single and double quotes.

practiceExercises.title: Two lines
practiceExercises.prompt: Use two print() statements so the output has exactly two lines: the first line says `Hello` and the second line says `World`.
practiceExercises.starterCode: # Print Hello on line 1 and World on line 2
hints: Every print() call ends with a newline automatically.
You need two separate print() statements — one for each line.

practiceExercises.title: Quotes inside a sentence
practiceExercises.prompt: Print this exact line: She said "Python is fun!". The double quotes must appear in the output.
practiceExercises.goal: Print a sentence that contains double-quote characters.
hints: Wrap the outer string in single quotes so the double quotes are treated as normal characters.
Or use a backslash (\") to escape the double quotes inside a double-quoted string.

practiceExercises.title: Mini ASCII art
practiceExercises.prompt:
Print a small 3-line picture. The output must be exactly:
*
**
***
practiceExercises.goal: Produce a 3-line triangle of stars using print().
hints: One print() per line of output.
Strings can have any characters inside the quotes — including `*`.
